entry.title: Re: SAP ECTR for SAP S/4HANA 1.2 - What’s new in the new major Release
entry.content:
Hi Team,

we have performed the SPS update on one of the SAP PLM Quality system after that we are  getting the ABAP programming error dume realted to DATA_OFFSET_NEGATIVE   

 

Below is the dump

==

Category ABAP programming error
Runtime Errors DATA_OFFSET_NEGATIVE
Except. CX_SY_RANGE_OUT_OF_BOUNDS
ABAP Program SAPLCV120
Application Component CA-DMS
Date and Time 01.07.2024 12:48:31

 

Short Text
Invalid subfield access: Offset negative

 

What happened?
Error in the ABAP application program.

The current ABAP program "SAPLCV120" had to be terminated because it found a
statement that could not be executed.

entry.title: Re: Fetching text from API_PRODUCT_SRV - Filter/key not working in Abap cloud call
entry.content:
Hi all,

Well, in the end we got it working but we decided to take this specific route: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/service-consumption-model-2-for-odata-client-proxy/ba-p/13573733

It is the service consumption model. 

I used this in the past and somehow I never got a entityset in my list on the left when I used a specific API with some stock info. Later on I discovered that the functions weren't available to us. Tried another api with api_product_srv an there you go, all the entities we needed. Followed the guide and that was it, works perfectly.

Cheers!

Laurens

entry.title: Load method (BAPI/ FM/ IDOC) to migrate WBS element Budget data
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to migrate WBS budget (including original budget, supplement, return, transfer, actual, plan data) cost posting from ERP system to S/4HANA. The table involved is RPSCO.

We are looking for a BAPI/ FM/ IDOC to migrate the data. 

Any lead to it would be appreciated.

Thank you so much in advance.

 

SAP Data Services FIN Cost Object Controlling ABAP Development SAP S/4HANA Cloud Data Migration

entry.title: Re: Restriction BP transaction (by BP Grouping)
entry.content:
@at-MartinS With this Standard object 'B_BUPA_GRP' ,you define which business partners can be edited on the basis of the authorization group. It does not help in restricting the access while creating the BP.

 

***This object is not needed if there is  special protection required for master data

Regards.

ZFiori.

entry.title: Ask the Speakers | SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 - Business AI Edition
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track 2024 - Business AI Edition's agenda is live!!!

Do you have questions for our Speakers? 

Here is what you can do 👇

- Go to www.sitblr.in 
- Find the topics that interest you from the following tracks

  • Track 1 - General SAP BTP and Other Products
  • Track 2 - Development Pro Code + Low Code
  • Track 3 - Data & Analytics + Integration
  • Track 4 - Artificial Intelligence + Automation
  • Track 5 - SAP UI5, Fiori, and Design
  • Track 6 - Concur
  • Track 7 - SAP (SF, CX)

- Return back to this post. 
- @tag the speakers and drop a question that you want them to address during the session.

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar: What's New in SAP Customer Checkout 2.0 FP19 & FP20
entry.content:
Hello,

keeping up with market trends is crucial in today's retail, sports and entertainment, and catering businesses. Continous development and enhancement of POS software are essential to stay up to date. 

We're happy to share with you our upcoming webinar 'What's New in SAP Customer Checkout 2.0 FP19 & FP20':

Date: July 25, 2024

Time: 1:30 - 2:30pm CET

Our SAP Customer Checkout experts will introduce you to the latest features of the new versions - covering both - SAP Customer Checkout, and the SAP Customer Checkout manager. You will also have the opportunity to ask any question you may have.

REGISTER HERE for the webinar.

We are looking forward to having you in this webinar!

Best regards,

Elena Vavitsa

Marketing Lead for SAP Customer Checkout

entry.title: Improving pricing performance in volume industries using the SAP Price Staging Hub by Vistex
entry.content:
Introduction 

In today's wholesale distribution industry, pricing has become a crucial topic. With the rise of online marketplaces and less loyal buyers, there is increased price transparency. At the same time, growing assortments, faster vendor price updates, and the importance of customer-specific pricing have led to a high volume of pricing information. Distributors now face challenges in maintaining prices, providing accurate customer-specific pricing across all channels in a performant way, and updating prices quickly. 

Furthermore, as the web-shop gains more importance, especially in industrial wholesale, customers using the web-shop expect to see their specific prices at all stages of browsing. However, the current method of calling the ERP backend synchronously to calculate the customer price leads to high load on the ERP system and potential waiting times for web-shop users. This is particularly challenging for high-volume industries such as wholesale distribution. 

To address these issues, SAP, in partnership with Vistex, has developed the SAP Price Staging Hub. This solution, based on the Business Technology Platform (BTP), provides pre-calculated prices for high-pricing volumes across distributed landscapes. It enables businesses to define and selectively process pricing-related changes, efficiently generating current price information and distributing time-sensitive customer-specific price data. The stored price data is easily accessible and can be consumed by other applications. Both push and pull scenarios for price publishing are possible. 

 Addressed Use Cases  

SAP Price Staging Hub by Vistex is designed to address several use cases: 

  1. E-commerce – highly efficient response to a large volume of simultaneous synchronous pricing requests from the web-shop for individual prices 
  1. Price Catalog – efficient generation of customer price catalogs, whether through scheduled tasks or manual requests 
  1. Requests from 3rd party applications – highly efficient response to significant amount of parallel synchronous pricing calls from external systems, such as pharmacy POS systems 

 Solution Overview 

SAP Price Staging Hub by Vistex is a BTP-based solution providing pre-calculated prices across distributed landscapes for very high pricing volumes.  

The solution provides businesses the ability to define and selectively process pricing-related changes, schedules and executes price calculations as needed, and stores the pre-determined prices for fast retrieval. It generates full or delta catalogs of final prices and publishes the predetermined prices to other systems. 

Solution in detail: 

Price data, business partner and material APIs deliver master data to the Price Staging Hub. Pricing procedures are replicated from SAP S/4HANA or SAP Business Suite.  

  1. Population 
    Price-related master data—including customer, product and price conditions—is fed to Price Staging Hub from the pricing source-of-truth.  This may be SAP S/4HANA or SAP Business Suite or another price management solution such as SAP Data Maintenance for S/4HANA by Vistex, pricing option or SAP Extended Price Management by Vistex.  Initially, a calculation of all relevant prices is executed, and the calculated prices are stored. 

  2. Detection of price changes 
    Re-determination of prices happens only when customer, product, and price conditions are changed. Price Staging Hub monitors price-related master data in the source system for changes that should trigger price re-determination. 

  3. Calculation 
    Price Staging Hub executes price re-calculations where needed, based on above mentioned master data changes, on a frequent schedule—measured in minutes or hours, not daily or weekly. 

  4. Price staging 
    Price Staging Hub stores the pre-determined prices and makes them readily available for: 
    - fast fetching via API (pull-scenario) by other solutions, 
    - publishing predetermined prices to other systems (push-scenario), 
    - generation of full or delta customer price catalogs.  

 Customer Value 

Implementing the SAP Price Staging Hub by Vistex offers several customer benefits. It improves the customer experience in the web-shop by showing individualized prices during browsing and in search result lists, with fast response times. It allows businesses to handle increasing transaction volumes on their web-shops over the next years. By providing current and accurate prices in real-time, it increases cart and RFQ conversion ratios. The processing times for creating and distributing customer price catalogs can be significantly reduced. 

Resources

Solution Brief – SAP Price Staging Hub by Vistex

entry.title: Queries around Cloud ready mode in sap MDG on S4 HANA 2023
entry.content:
Hello All,

 

Our system is being upgraded to S/4 Hana 2023 on premise. We are planning to implement a Cloud Ready Mode for Business partner (Customer and Supplier). I do have a few queries about it.

 

  1. Currently we are using Ariba SLP for onboarding Suppliers. Is MDG Cloud Ready Mode supports Ariba SLP integration?
  2. How derivation and validation across entity types works in Cloud-ready mode. Do single-entity and across-entity Badis (USMD_RULE_SERVICE) are supported in Cloud-ready mode?
  3. In Automation workflow can system trigger the validation at request submission stage, or a separate step would be required in the workflow? In the current process (Rube base workflow) it does the validation at the Change Request submission stage.
  4. How can we enable a set of fields at each workflow steps (e.g. four level of approvers) in automation workflow?
  5. Can you please provide the product (Material) release date for support in Cloud Ready Mode?
  6. In product/Material Cloud Ready Mode- Is classic classification will be supported for material class (001) in classic mode?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Master Data   @0012anirban @Mark63  @Rahul15 

 

Thanks

Rahul Khandelwal

entry.title: Re: UnexpectedEOFAtTarget issue while calling SAP SuccessFactors OData from SAP CPI
entry.content: were you able to figure this error out

entry.title: Output should be processed when Invoice is approved in ZATCA (edoc_cockpit)
entry.content:
Hi All

The client requirement is system should process ALE output ( medium-A) after invoice is approved in EDOC-COCKPIT portal in SA.

Business use VF31 to process these outputs.

After creating the invoice in VF01/Vf04 it will be sent to EDOC_COKPIT portal shown below and the requirement is ZRD output type should be processed only when the Billing document is approved in EDOC_COKPIT.

I was thinking to develop a VOFM output routine based on above conditions and assigned that to ZRD output type in Output procedure so system will check the Billing doc status in EDOC-COCKPIT and if not approved it should not be determined the ZRD in billing document.

But I see the above solution may not work as after creating the billing doc it is sent to EDOC-COCKPIT after some time someone manfully approved it. So in this case there is a time gap.

What i am thinking we need to write a code in VF31 program where system should consider those invoices which are approved in EDOC-COKCPIT into VF31 list?

Tanks in advance

 

 

 

 

As per ZATCA compliance any

entry.title: Issue in Creating CSV file on FTP folder from SAP internal table data via background job program
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am getting the issue/error in Creating CSV file on FTP folder from SAP internal table data via background job program.

Background Job is failing.

Connection is fine via FM 'FTP_CONNECT'.

Program in Foreground is working fine!

Please help me out with the FM's/solution.

Thanks in Advance.

AJ

entry.title: BAR CODE CREATION IN SAP Public cloud
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I am currently seeking a solution in SAP Public Cloud for generating barcodes based on Material code, Plant, and Batch Number during Goods Receipt processes.

Once the barcode is generated, it needs to be sent to an external barcode machine for printing.

During Goods Issue processes, the scanner should be able to read the barcode, display the material in MIGO, and issue the material accordingly.

I would like to know if this process can be achieved using Standard SAP functionalities or if customization is required.

entry.title: Inventory cost adjustment
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We maintained the Inventory Cost wrongly and would like to update the Cost as zero yen. However, the Inventory Valuation will be posted to P&L if we change the Inventory Cost directly.
Is there any way to fix the Inventory Cost without affecting P&L?

Or should we cancel all the related transactions, adjust the Inventory Cost as zero and then repost those transactions?

 

Thank you and appreciate for your help.

 

Best Regards,

Michelle

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified Professional - SAP Enterprise Architect - Preparation Tips
entry.content:
@mahesh_sardesai 

I just passed SAP EA certification exam, my feeling is that the concepts of TOGAF are quite helpful for the exam.

Based on the concepts of TOGAF10,  Architecture Roadmap & Implementation and Migration Plan will be drafted in Phase E (Opportunities & Solutions Phase) as inputs in Phase F (Migration Planning). The official Architecture Roadmap & Implementation and Migration Plan will be finalized in Phase F.

Hope this comment is helpful.

entry.title: Re: Queries around Cloud ready mode in sap MDG on S4 HANA...
entry.content:
Hi Rahul,

Please refer to the cloud-ready mode documents for your below queries.

https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/10/129fb876-917e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

For the product master, the details are available on the Roadmap Explorer.

https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73555000100800002492&PRODUCT=901B0ED1A0641EDA9FF75276E440C0EF&range=CURRENT-2025Q4#Q3%202023

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: How to find a Text table, if it exists
entry.content:
Hi,

Go to to the SE11 T-code entry table (DD08L). The table text in the database table is not visible here. 

 

Right now Enter the same table (DD08L) in the SE16N T-code to view the fields that are hidden in the SE11 Database table. however, as shown in the SE16N T-code below.

How to determine which table the concealed fields are from and then proceed with the SE11 T-code Enter the information in the table as indicated above, then proceed as indicated below.

The outcome may be seen in the image below. It includes the table and field that were hidden in the previous table.

Regards,

Awais Syed.

entry.title: From Grief to Growth: How Hamza’s Perseverance Helped Him Become an SAP Professional
entry.content:
When his father died, Hamza stepped up and out of his comfort zone to be the best big brother and father figure he could be. “It was then when I realized that I should never stop learning new things and having new experiences in my personal and professional life. I knew I had to keep moving forward to be successful and to be a role model,” the 28-year-old from Tunisia explains. “Central to my ethos is the belief that knowledge is the cornerstone of success. Thus, I am perpetually in pursuit of learning, seeking to broaden my horizons and enhance my skills.” And that is when he stumbled upon SAP. 

Immediately hooked by SAP learning resources

“It all began with a quest to find the appropriate software,” Hamza remembers. “As I explored various options, I stumbled upon SAP and was captivated not only by the wealthy resources available online but also by the miscellaneous features that the learning resources provided.” Hamza was immediately hooked. He took a deep dive into the world of SAP, went from beginner level courses to implementing SAP solutions and specializing in consultancy. Up to this day, he has completed more than 20 programs, earned records of achievements and certifications proving his valuable knowledge – and making his resume more than respectable. However, Hamza still was not content. 

“Through this journey, I honed my skills in client engagement, solution design, and hands-on implementation,” Hamza says.“My thirst for knowledge persisted. I yearned to comprehend the entire consulting process comprehensively, from inception to completion,” he explains in hindsight. And then he stumbled over SAP again. To be precise, it was the “Becoming an SAP Professional” course on Coursera, one of the world’s largest online learning platforms, that caught his attention. Being part of an online, seven-course SAP Technology Consultant Professional Certificate, Hamza could learn even more about SAP, the role of a technology consultant, and the value SAP professionals bring to customers. “Through this journey, I honed my skills in client engagement, solution design, and hands-on implementation,” Hamza says. “The pinnacle of this education endeavor was a practical project where I applied my expertise to create solutions tailored to real business needs.” And it paid off quickly. 

Now he shares his knowledge and skills with others

Just recently, Hamza embraced a new role as a Content Creator, through "Let's Make Tunisia Better, “ a program aimed at promoting knowledge sharing and professional development in Tunisia, where he immerses himself in the complexities of functional consulting, honing his skills and expertise with every Topics. “It's not just a role, it’s a journey where each challenge becomes an opportunity for growth and innovation,” he says, convinced that without his latest certification, this would not have been that easy. 

“For those aspiring to craft a strategic SAP career development plan, I wholeheartedly recommend prioritizing this certificate. It not only refines your skills but also prepares you for the upcoming chapters in your professional journey,” Hamza states from his own experience: “The pivotal moment in my life is to secure the position of an SAP consultant. Given my responsibilities towards my family, this achievement will not just be a career milestone but a dream to fulfill. I owe this accomplishment to my perseverance and, significantly, to my certificates.” 

Start your own success story today

Did you feel inspired by Hamza’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories!

entry.title: Receiving error while adding new ABAP Cloud Project in Eclipse using Service Key
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm getting the below error while adding new ABAP Cloud project in Eclipse using the service key.

Can you please help me out. 

Note:- I've tried deleting the Instance and re-creating the instance and service key , Even this not help me.

Thanks, 

Praveen

entry.title: I am unable to submit data using planning in SAP BW via Analysis Office
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I'm currently trying to use planning as an input method via Analysis Office in SAP Business Warehouse. However, I encountered an issue when the input data exceeds 250 characters. The info object used as a key figure only supports a maximum length of 250 characters (CHAR).

From my understanding, only info objects set as key figures can be used for input data in query planning. Unfortunately, I can't use fields with SSTRING, which have a maximum length of 1333 characters, nor can I use the long text setting on the info object. This is because planning only reads the key of the info object, not its description.

Do you have any suggestions or workarounds for a requirement to input data using planning in Analysis Office when the data exceeds 250 characters?

Regards,
Faisal

BW (SAP Business Warehouse) SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP BW/4HANA SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office

entry.title: Re: Cleansing of iContent server, data report download, SF LMS
entry.content:
Hi,

I have the same need. 

I do not have any easy and direct way to see folder size, I do however go by:

  • content type: does it contain video ?
  • Last launch date

These are enough to identify the big space eaters. A video is quickly 500 Mb, a well built scorm is more likely 20 to 50 Mb. 

I am interested in any other ideas or input !

Cheers,

Kris

entry.title: SAP Enable Now Honored with Prestigious Award at the 19th Annual 2024 Globee® Awards for Technology
entry.content:
SAP Enable Now is delighted to announce that it has been recognized as a winner at the 19th Annual 2024 Globee® Awards for Technology, one of the world’s premier business awards programs. SAP Enable Now was awarded in the category of Digital Experience Platforms for its achievement of outstanding digital experience for all business users to foster adoption of software solutions.

Explore the complete list of 2024 winners here: https://globeeawards.com/technology/winners/

The Globee® Awards for Technology honor and celebrate the achievements and positive contributions of organizations and individuals from all over the world. This award reflects SAP Enable Now’s commitment to innovation, excellence, and the continued development of cutting-edge solutions in the technology sector.

“We are incredibly proud to receive this recognition from the Globee® Awards,” said Vikas Kapoor, Head and Vice President of SAP Enable Now. “This award is a testament to the persistent hard work and dedication of our team and reinforces our vision to deliver the best assistance for our business users in their business transformation journey.”

The Globee® Awards, which highlight achievements in various industries, are known for their rigorous judging process. The judging panel includes industry experts from a wide range of fields, ensuring that each entry is evaluated fairly and comprehensively.

As a winner of the Globee® Awards for Technology, SAP Enable Now joins a distinguished group of companies that are leading the way in innovation and technology. This award will inspire us to continue pushing the boundaries and delivering exceptional value to our customers and stakeholders.

SAP Enable Now is a powerful tool that underscores the importance of digital adoption and business transformation. It's a sophisticated digital experience platform designed to facilitate smooth software adoption, drive operational efficiency, and spur digital maturity. It extends a comprehensive, integrated knowledge transfer solution that accelerates end-user adoption of new technology, ultimately fueling business transformation. As businesses become increasingly digital, SAP Enable Now plays a pivotal role in promoting digital literacy and cultivating a versatile, tech-savvy workforce that leverages technology to its maximum potential. Hence, it's not just a learning tool, but an essential catalyst for digital transformation and a contributor to enhanced digital experiences.

“Congratulations to all the exceptional winners of the 2024 Globee Awards for Technology,” said San Madan, President of Globee Awards. “Your groundbreaking innovations and unwavering dedication have set a new standard of excellence in technologies everywhere. At Globee Awards, we proudly celebrate your remarkable achievements and look forward to witnessing the transformative impact you will continue to make in the world. Keep pushing the boundaries of what’s possible and inspiring us all.”

Over 2,145 judges from around the world, representing a diverse array of industry experts, applied for the judging process. The judges are listed here: https://globeeawards.com/technology/judges/

About SAP Enable Now: 
SAP Enable Now is a leading digital adoption platform, specializing in delivering a comprehensive digital learning asset management solution with versatile delivery channels, including SAP Companion as in-app assistant default in many SAP Cloud applications. With a commitment to excellence, we strive to exceed customer expectations through innovative solutions and exceptional service.

About the Globee Awards 
Globee Awards are conferred in ten programs and competitions: the Globee® Awards for American Business, Globee® Awards for Artificial Intelligence, Globee® Awards for Business, Globee® Awards for Customer Excellence, Globee® Awards for Cybersecurity, Globee® Awards for Disruptors, Golden Bridge Awards®, Globee® Awards for Leadership, Globee® Awards for Technology, and the Globee® Awards for Women In Business. Learn more about the Globee Awards at https://globeeawards.com

All trademarks belong to their respective owners.

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content: Thank you @Kunal77 This can be of help to many.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Delete Activated Request in BW/4HANA - Master Data Deletion Error
entry.content: Hi! This note solve the problem: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003276986

entry.title: Re: How to Call an iFlow from SAP CAP Java?
entry.content:
While there might be options to simplify the code when it comes to the response handling (I'm less familiar with Java), conceptually this should work.

As long as your application (also in hybrid mode) is bound to the Destination service, and the correct destination name is used it should be able to find it. Then it's about making sure your endpoint to the iflow is correct as the url you posted seems incorrect to me.

Double check the exposed endpoint mentioned in the deployed iflow: the url will probably follow a syntax similar to: https://<tenant>.it-cpi018-rt.cfapps.eu10-003.hana.ondemand.com/http/<endpoint>.

entry.title: How to use user exit EXIT_SAPLIEDI_004
entry.content:
Dear SAP friends,

I am struggling to write proper coding in user exit EXIT_SAPLIEDI_004. The intention is to read a specific idoc segment en move a field of that segment into the export value segment_text. I have found that by modifying segment_text, i can achieve my goal. However, the user exit is entered for each line item of the financial document during inbound invoice processing, and the current line item is not specified. Therefore, i cannot read the correct segment of the idoc and fill segment_text. Please advice.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Contents of internal table in transfer correctly using FTP FM
entry.content: Please share the solution on this issue.

entry.title: SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP Custom events
entry.content:
Dear all,

Do anyone know if it is possible to create custom events in SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP? I can only find the built-in events. I want to get notified when my stock goes below our safety stock threshold from SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud. Any help here would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements default filter with SelectionVariant n...
entry.content:
Hello All, 

If someone is still searching for a solution, you need to define the annotation in the manifest file. This worked for me:

Described here:

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/49a6ba5b8d6946208322a9f7e16837c2

Regards,

Bettina

entry.title: Recording and slide deck of SAP on IBM i June webcast available for download
entry.content:
In our SAP on IBM i Webcast "Establishing Trust with Certificates and TLS" on June 26th, 2024, Thom Haze explained the general concepts of Transport Layer Security (TLS) and certificates. He introduced certificate and application management on IBM i, using the Digital Certificate Manager (DCM). Afterwards, Christian Bartels gave an overview over the TLS support with various components of the SAP Business Suite and explained how to configure TLS in SAP applications.

As usual, the recording and slide deck of the webcast have been made available for download at the link http://ibm.biz/RadioSAPIBMi. You can find it there in the folder 2024, subfolder June 2024. If you missed the webcast or want to look at some aspects in more detail, you can look at the provided material and watch the recording at your own speed.

The link is accessible by invitation only. If you are not yet a user of Box, it will be necessary to register (free of charge) at Box with the company email address that will be used for the download. If you have previously downloaded recordings or slide decks of SAP on IBM i webcasts from this link, you should already be authorized to it. If this is the first time you are trying to download material from this link, or if your access was lost for whatever reason, you can request access by sending an email to ibmsapevents@ibm.com and providing your name, company name, country, requested company email address (if different from sender), as well as the requested webcast date and title.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Great to have you joining in from Chicago @Dan-Crawley - for info: SAP Champion Fausto Motter is based in Chicago - maybe you can connect in SAP Community or at a future meetup! 
Looking forward to seeing more interactions from you in SAP Community!

@FMotter

entry.title: Re: Receiving error while adding new ABAP Cloud Project i...
entry.content:
The error message indicates that you successfully authenticated at the identity authentication service (= you probably you saw a success message in the browser). But afterwards the logon to the ABAP service instance (= ABAP system) failed. As the error message indicates that happened probably because you don't have a user (CB user / ABAP user) in the system.

Please see note 3254529: https://me.sap.com/notes/3254529

The note contains links to tutorials on user setup, roles, etc.

entry.title: Re: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills / Solutions and Templates...
entry.content: That is the system provided through the "hands-on practice" section of the learning journey, right? Then you should be able to see the templates and solutions in that system. We did not rename anything. I just re-activated my own subscription and confirmed that the /LRN/-objects are there. Have you tried adding package /LRN/S4D401_EXERCISE to the list of Favorite Packages (as described in the first exercise of the "Intermediate ABAP Programming" part of the learning journey?

entry.title: S4H aATP - Is "check level" capability available for SD Delivery Scheduling documents ?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we are investigating to replace our currently used APO-GATP solution (online ATP and BOP) using S4H-aATP.  Today we use the GATP setting "check level" (in SCM/APO to find in SPRO: APO>GATP>BOP->Define CheckLevel) to allow SD Delivery Scheduling Documents to be ATP-checked on Schedule Line level (and not item level like normal Sales Orders). Is this capability available in S4H-aATP meanwhile or is there a respective solution provided  by SAP ?

Regards

Thomas

entry.title: VOFM in SD and BTE in FICO
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

1.Why SAP has VOFM only for SD ? Why we can't achieve the same functionality using User-Exits or BADI or Enhancement Framework ?

2.Why do we have BTE's only for FICO ?

I had checked with couple of my Seniors but was not able to get a satisfying reply. Hence thought of checking this with the experts in SCN.

Kindly share your expertise on these two queries.

Thanks,
K.Kiran.

entry.title: Re: How to Call an iFlow from SAP CAP Java?
entry.content:
Hi Willem,

The url is correct because the message that i am getting, Failed to get destination., is coming from the catch block only.

 

catch (DestinationNotFoundException | DestinationAccessException e) {
            return e.getMessage();
        }

 

But even after correctly binding my app with the destination, it is not finding it. 

 

**The url that you have mentioned is of the iFlow which i want to hit from the cap app.I have mentioned the iflow url in the destination correctly.

entry.title: Re: When creating a Purcahasing Catalog, is it possible t...
entry.content:
Dear User,

The Product Catalog Management business scenario allows your company to use catalogs to centrally manage non-stock materials that are required for use such as office materials, engineering sample materials, computer systems, or machines. The scenario is not for products to be held on stock since they can be procured directly for an account assignment.

You can create two types of catalogs.
Supplier Catalog:
Create a supplier catalog that contains products from just one supplier, using the imported BMEcat or CSV file sent to you by your supplier. In the Product Portfolio work center - Catalogs view - There is a button called - Open catalog file template - This will download the template to upload the catalogs.

Custom Catalog:
Create a custom catalog manually that contains products from several suppliers

You can refer to help documents below.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2de0371b722d101498258283cde79b78.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/392ee36a5fc2411d9c3b44abaefc894f.html?locale=en-US

Hope this helps. 

Best Regards,
Arun

Thank you in advance, 

Regards 

Shreyas.

entry.title: A New Place to Exchange - Community Events
entry.content:
I'm thrilled to offer you an excellent opportunity to connect with people who share your interests, ask questions, and address any concerns you might have about the next on your list event in the dedicated to this event exchange group.

We invite you to actively participate and become an integral member of our diverse and inclusive community. Here's how you can find your next event group and start building meaningful connections:




 

 

 

Check our Community Events rights now!

entry.title: Re: How to replace RRW3_GET_QUERY_VIEW_DATA

Hi Martin,

regards, Martin

entry.title: Re: Blog Series: SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition – 02 TM Freight Accounting and Material Valuation
entry.content:
Hello, 

Thank you very much for your blog, where you give a great description of inbound delivery in SAP TM.
I have a requirement from a client to post goods receipt and fright accurals at the same time. ( On a one click ) is it possible to link these two processes in SAP Public cloud somehow? Could you please help me?
( I need to eliminate time gap between post goods receipt and posting freight accurals )

entry.title: Customer and Vendor Clearing process
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm looking for best practices for managing the vendor and customer clearing process in SAP, particularly when dealing with multiple currencies and handling prepayments/advance payments. Our organization often makes and receives payments in various currencies and repayments/advance payments we need an efficient way to manage these transactions to ensure accurate clearing.

Specifically, I would like to know:

  1. Best Practices:

    • What are the best practices for setting up and managing vendor and customer clearing in SAP?
    • How should we handle transactions in multiple currencies to ensure accurate clearing and reconciliation?
    • What is the recommended approach for dealing with prepayments and advance payments?

  2. Automation:

    • How can we automate the vendor and customer clearing process in SAP to minimize manual intervention and reduce errors?
    • Are there specific SAP tools or functionalities (such as F110, F-44, F-32) that can help streamline this process?
    • What are the key configurations and settings required to enable effective automation?

  3. Integration and Reporting:

    • How can we integrate the clearing process with our financial reporting to ensure accurate reflection of cleared and uncleared items?
    • Are there any specific reports or dashboards within SAP that can help monitor and manage the clearing process effectively?

Any insights, detailed steps, or resources you can share would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now Web Assistant - Working with extended content
entry.content:
Hello Kristina, 

thanks a lot for such an exhaustive blog.

I have just included the extended Workarea to the new Workarea and the "Content - Server" is currently in Unsorted Objects. I tried dragging it to "root group" on Manager, however without success. As a result, I tried opening Producer and as you stated, it takes really long. 

Do you have any ideas how I could work this out? (I also tried "Start Editing All Objects" from Manager but nothing changes when I clicked the button).

Thanks a lot in advance for your help!

entry.title: Clearing process
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm looking for best practices for managing the vendor and customer clearing process in SAP, particularly when dealing with multiple currencies and handling prepayments/advance payments. Our organization often makes and receives payments in various currencies and we need an efficient way to manage these transactions to ensure accurate clearing.

Specifically, I would like to know:

  1. Best Practices:

    • What are the best practices for setting up and managing vendor and customer clearing in SAP?
    • How should we handle transactions in multiple currencies to ensure accurate clearing and reconciliation?
    • What is the recommended approach for dealing with prepayments and advance payments?

  2. Automation:

    • How can we automate the vendor and customer clearing process in SAP to minimize manual intervention and reduce errors?
    • Are there specific SAP tools or functionalities (such as F110, F-44, F-32) that can help streamline this process?
    • What are the key configurations and settings required to enable effective automation?

  3. Integration and Reporting:

    • How can we integrate the clearing process with our financial reporting to ensure accurate reflection of cleared and uncleared items?
    • Are there any specific reports or dashboards within SAP that can help monitor and manage the clearing process effectively?

Any insights, detailed steps, or resources you can share would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - FS items
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

FS items can be added, and mapped to a G/L account (via apps "Map FS Items with G/L Accounts" and "Assign FS Item Mappings") later after transaction data in accounting have been posted, as long as the “Release Universal Journal” data monitor task has not been executed yet. Or after the mapping, re-execute the “Release UJ” task to load data from ACDOCA to ACDOCU.

Here's more information:

Financial Statement Items:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/33e57a9f475c4aab8748e178af0f2b7c.html?locale=en-US

Integration with G/L Accounts:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/68c83a5cfb1c47589752769a2878b91a.html?locale=en-US

Map FS Items with G/L Accounts:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/7a771b681e2741e387680f274f6fb5f8.html?locale=en-US

Assign FS Item Mappings:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/bbbf971be966408c92a50871065da227.html?locale=en-US

Release Universal Journal: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/9cba307a1d56482abb96f51e545e0863.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: XML parser error in receiver determination in pipeline concept while testing with Csv, pdf file.
entry.content: I don't think that your error is pipeline related, best is you switch on the trace to find out where exactly the error happens, it might be that your payload is wrong

entry.title: Re: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills / Solutions and Templates...
entry.content: Thank you so much for your help! Yes, it seems, I wasn`t on the correct learning landscape. Now I can access the available content. 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to create RFC function module and how to call this function module
entry.content:
Hi,

How to create the multiple range select option in RFC.

After constructing the structure, create a range ZT_RANGE for multiple selection.

*--------Added by Awais Syed-----------*

*------Ranges for Company Code BUKRS------*
LOOP AT lt_bukrs INTO ls_bukrs.
IF ls_bukrs-low IS NOT INITIAL AND ls_bukrs-high IS INITIAL.
r_bukrs-sign = 'I'.
r_bukrs-option = 'EQ'.
r_bukrs-low = ls_bukrs-low.
r_bukrs-high = ls_bukrs-high.
APPEND r_bukrs.
ELSEIF ls_bukrs-low IS NOT INITIAL AND ls_bukrs-high IS NOT INITIAL.
r_bukrs-sign = 'I'.
r_bukrs-option = 'BT'.
r_bukrs-low = ls_bukrs-low.
r_bukrs-high = ls_bukrs-high.
APPEND r_bukrs.
ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.
*-----Ended Ranges for Company code BUKRS------*

Regards,

Awais Syed.

Thanks,

Rupesh

entry.title: Re: Data Binding with Repeating Items and Simple Tables in Print Forms
entry.content:
Thank you for the very informative article. I have one related question.

For example, is it possible to display two or more groups of line items within a delivery note? The idea is to split the delivery note in half and display the same line items in the top and bottom sections.

I downloaded the standard Delivery Note template from the demo environment, duplicated the table containing the default line items, and checked the data binding, but it didn't work well.

If anyone knows how to do this, I would appreciate it if you could let me know. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Mass Maintenance for Characteristic values
entry.content: Try using Standard T.Code MASS_CHARVAL to mass maintain Retail characteristic.

entry.title: Re: Error while doing the AP invoice based on the GRPO -...
entry.content: Since document is in local currency, I presume you are using a different system currency? Check GL Account determination setup, set exchange rate accounts for purchasing, general and inventory.

entry.title: how to get certificate to web service api
entry.content:
Hi

I created a WEB SERVICE
which addresses the URL: 

https://data.gov.il/api/action/datastore_search?resource_id=5c78e9fa-c2e2-4771-93ff-7f400a12f7ba&limit=999999 

This address is directed to receive data from GOV

When I run the call to web service 
I get the following error:
"<html><head><title>Application Server Error</title></head><body>\r\n<H2><b>500 Native SSL error</b></H2><br>\ nError is logged with Tag: {001cf7ff}<br><hr>Tue Jul 2 05:15:01 2024</body></html>"

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2748696 
I tried these steps:
https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2748696

Attached is a photo of the manual I tried to solve the problem
The question is for which URL do I need to create a certificate to receive the data required for SERVICE

what are the steps i need to do in order to get cartificate?
To which url do I need to create the certificate?
but without success to solve the matter
Do you have any idea how to solve this
I would greatly appreciate your help

entry.title: Re: Empty sales area BP tcode
entry.content: Hi, The solution is SAP note 2393665 - BP_CVI: Missing display of the sales area when selecting a role grouping of the business partner: https://me.sap.com/notes/2393665/E

Yann

entry.title: Re: SyntaxError: no closing braces found in '{' after pos:0 (at BindingParser-dbg.js:409:10)
entry.content: Have you opened an issue at https://github.com/SAP/openui5/issues ? From my experience this is best to get such poblems fixed

entry.title: Re: Lineside replenishment SAP WM
entry.content:
Hello @SaleemSk 

I would suggest to use standard WM-PP Interface and set up the staging indicator to 4 "Manual Staging" in control cycles. The numbers of the storage bins at work centers are defined in the control cycles too. 

Then when a component is backflushed, it is taken directly from a bin defined in a control cycle. LE-WM won't create a transfer requirement for such a good issue and no further actions are needed in LE-WM.

As the staging indicator is 4 "Manual Staging", you can move components to the bins at work centers manually or you can use Replenishment for Fixed Storage Bins process.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: How to Reduce Database DTU on SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Database is a critical component for the scalability of SAP Commerce Cloud.

In this article, we will outline a methodology to reduce database utilization and enhance system scalability when the database is the bottleneck.

This methodology consists of three straightforward steps, each of which we will explain in detail:

  1. Monitor the Database Consumption: Assess the current database usage to establish a baseline before optimization.
  2. Identify Top Waits: Determine the primary causes of the contention to prioritize areas for improvement.
  3. Identify Top Queries: Pinpoint the most resource-intensive queries to target for optimization.
  4. Drill down to the Code Level: Examine the Java stack trace to identify the code to optimize.

 

Monitor the Database Consumption

You can access DTU usage with Dynatrace. For this analysis, we will follow these steps:

  • Go to Observe and Explore > Dashboards > SAP – Infra Usage dashboard
  • Go to DB Usage - DTU graph and click Configure in data explorer

  • Configure the timeframe

  • Change the resolution to 1 min
  • Hide Used (max) metrics

For the analysis, we use Used (average) metric with 1 min resolution. By default, the resolution may be higher (ex: 1 hour) and you may miss spikes using the average, this is why we change to 1 min. Please note Dynatrace will keep only up to 15 days of history with such resolution.

Used (max) metric can be very confusing for the analysis and we do not recommend using this metric for the analysis. It is normal for the database to utilize the maximum available hardware for short periods. When a spike lasts less than a minute, it is called a micro-spike, and it does not impact the system's overall performance. If you use a long resolution and rely on the max metric, even a single micro-spike per hour can make the graph appear alarming when this is not a concern.

The objective here is to drill down to the period of time where DTU is too high to identify the shortest period of time where we should focus for the next step.

Identify Top Waits

DTU (Database Transaction Unit) is a measure used by Azure SQL Database to describe the relative capacity of a database. It is an abstraction that encapsulates a combination of CPU, memory, reads, and writes. Here is a breakdown of the resources behind DTU:

  • CPU (Compute) - The amount of CPU resources available for processing queries and operations.

  • Memory - The amount of RAM allocated for data caching, query execution, and other in-memory processes.

  • Data IO - The throughput of read operations from disk, which affects the speed at which data can be read into memory.

  • Log IO - The throughput of write operations to the transaction log, which impacts the speed at which transactions can be committed.

DTUs provide a simplified way to measure and scale the performance of Azure SQL Databases, allowing you to choose a performance level without having to worry about individual resource allocation.

When analyzing a potential bottleneck, it is crucial to identify which resource is limiting the system's performance.

In most of the cases, the resource contention will be the CPU (usually for frontend traffic) but in some cases it could be Data IO or Write IO (usually due to jobs).

To identify the contention, we will follow these steps:

  • Connect to hAC
  • Go to Console > Flexible Search > SQL query
  • Execute the following direct SQL query

 

SELECT
    end_time,
    avg_cpu_percent AS 'Average CPU Utilization In Percent',
    avg_data_io_percent AS 'Average Data IO In Percent',
    avg_log_write_percent AS 'Average Log Write I/O Throughput Utilization In Percent',
    avg_memory_usage_percent AS 'Average Memory Usage In Percent'
FROM sys.dm_db_resource_stats 
ORDER BY end_time ASC;

 

Below is an example of result. From this, you can identify the resource that is using the highest percentage of DTU.

You may want to copy to Excel and graph the result for better visualization.

Note: The query on dm_db_resource_stats captures data every 15 sec for a period of one hour.
Unfortunately you cannot analyse for further in the past so if you do not have the data but you can probably consider that your main contention is the CPU.

 

Identify Top Queries

To identify the top queries for a certain period of time, we will use hAC and follow these steps:

  • Connect to hAC
  • Go to Console > Flexible Search > SQL query
  • Execute the following direct SQL query after replacing start time and end time in UTC

 

SELECT TOP 10
    p.query_id query_id,
    qt.query_sql_text query_sql_text,
    ROUND(SUM(count_executions * avg_cpu_time / 1000.0),2) AS total_cpu_millisec,
    ROUND(SUM(avg_logical_io_reads),2) total_logic_read_IO,
    ROUND(SUM(avg_logical_io_writes),2) total_logic_write_IO,
    ROUND(CONVERT(float, SUM(rs.avg_duration*rs.count_executions))*0.001,2) total_duration,
    SUM(rs.count_executions) count_executions,  
    MAX(round(rs.max_cpu_time/1000,2)) as max_cpu_time_ms,
    AVG(round([rs].[avg_duration]/1000,2))as avg_duration_ms_sec,
    COUNT(distinct p.plan_id) num_plans 
FROM sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs
    JOIN sys.query_store_plan p ON p.plan_id = rs.plan_id
    JOIN sys.query_store_query q ON q.query_id = p.query_id
    JOIN sys.query_store_query_text qt ON q.query_text_id = qt.query_text_id
WHERE NOT (rs.last_execution_time < '2024-07-01 4:00:00' OR rs.first_execution_time > '2024-07-01 5:00:00')
GROUP BY p.query_id, qt.query_sql_text, q.object_id
HAVING COUNT(distinct p.plan_id) >= 1
ORDER BY total_cpu_millisec DESC

 

Here is an example of result

In the example, we order by total_cpu_millisec but you can also order by total_logic_read_IO or total_logic_write_IO depending of your contention analysed at the previous step.

Note: Why not using Top database statements metric provided by Dynatrace? Top database statements is quite useful to identify top queries that impact the total response time of the application. But for Dynatrace the database is a black-box and it does not help to identify top queries per the resource that creates the contention. We need an internal view for this to make sure for focus on the right queries to optimize.

 

Drill down to the Code Level

Now we're going to use Dynatrace to identify the code that is causing the execution of a particular query.

  • Go to Application Observability > Database Services
  • On the top level, select the time window of the analysis

Note: In Dynatrace, this is normal to see two databases. This comes from the fact there are two gateways and Dynatrace identifies them as two different database even if they have the same name. By selecting Top database statements, you group the executions of each databases together.

  • Click on Top database statements
  • Click on the Filter requests search field, select Database statement and search for your query

  • Here is an example of result

Note: This is normal to have the query duplicated as Dynatrace identifies two databases

Note: Dynatrace records only the most frequent queries so you may not find your query if this is slow but executed few times only.

  • Click Actions > ... > Service Backtrace

  • Select the web application (ex: yacceleratorstorefront)
  • Check "Requests from" section to understand what are the HTTP requests calling this request (if require you can filter for a specific HTTP request)

Note: The incoming requests on the left represents the number of HTTP requests and the outgoing calls on the right represents the number of query executions. 

  • Click on Stacktrace tab and drill down to understand what is the code calling this query

Note: This tab filters HTTP threads for the selected query. Unfortunately as HTTP threads are part of a pool and reused, it will include any stack traces that executes queries for these threads.

Note: Code-level data is only available for 5 days.

 

Conclusion

After identifying the top query and the corresponding code executing it, focus on optimizing the query or reducing the frequency of its execution. By systematically repeating this process for each query that significantly consumes resources, you will likely achieve a substantial reduction in your DTU usage.

entry.title: Equivalent fiori app for F-05 in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Does anyone knows which app used for posting with the same logic in F-05? 

When apps searched related to F-05 in fiori app library, sap gives "Clear G/L Accounts" and "Post General Journal Entries" as referance apps but we need to post also to AP, AR as we did in F-05 in GUI.

Any help?

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget Calls a SAP CPI REST API - Failed with CORS Error
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently trying to make a call to the SAP CPI I-Flow via an SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget.

The I-Flow is triggered via REST-API and everything works so far via Postman with Basic Authentication via ClientID + ClientSecret.

Unfortunately, the REST-API call fails when I try it from the SAP Analytics Cloud/Javascript.

Here you can see my current I-Flow Settings in the REST-API: 

 

Here you can see my current coding in SAP Analytics Cloud/Javascript:

AJAX Call: 

$.ajax({
  url: apiUrl,
  type: "POST",
  headers: {
    "Authorization": "Basic " + btoa(username) + ":" + btoa(password)
  }
})
  .done(function (data){
  $.each(data, function (key, value){

  })
})
  .fail(function (jqXHR, textStatus){
  alert("Error: " + textStatus);
})


I get the following error when executing:

it has something to do with Cors Policy, but i dont know exactly how to handle this problem. 

I also tested it with the Fetch Call but the same error appears. 

Can you help me here what my error could be?

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Best regards

Chris.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Yes Key Users can create default app settings for filters, tables, a
entry.content:
Hi Jocelyn,

The blog is really great. Thanks for you efforts.

But I'd like to ask about a problem with filter values. First of all I would like to say that with previous SAP versions I was able to Adapt the UI of my Analytical List Pages, create multiple views and set different values for particular Compact or Visual filters. The filter values were persisted, and I was able to move the views to next systems without issues.

After upgrading to SAP FIORI FES FOR S/4HANA 2023 (with SAPUI5 1.120.15) I'm no longer able to persist filter values. This is valid for both custom and SAP standard Fiori ALP apps. The values are simply not saved in the content of /UIF/LREPDCONT* tables.

I'm following strictly the guides, which say that the filter should be set in "UI Adaptation" mode, but this doesn't make any difference.

Adapting SAP Fiori List Reports works fine.

Any suggestions how to fix Analytical List Pages with SAP FES 2023?

entry.title: Re: Queries around Cloud ready mode in sap MDG on S4 HANA...
entry.content: See also the FAQ page about cloud-ready mode in SAP Master Data Governance on S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: Dependent Item Amount Issue
entry.content:
Hi Prateek,

you should check your calculation key in combination of the incoming data in the BIT.

In the Transaction of the calculation key you will find a little help. It is the simulation button. By this you can check directly if the given combination makes sense and works correct. In the most of the case if you get a year it's the combination of date/currency and the debit/credit option.

Best regards

Jörg Luther

entry.title: Re: Receiving error while adding new ABAP Cloud Project i...
entry.content:
Hi Armin,

Thanks for your response.

As mentioned in the note 3254529, I had gone through the tutorial. In the tutorial it is mentioned to launch the BTP cockpit as admin and maintain/create employee and create business user and add developer role to it. As mentioned in the above steps, i did login in the BTP cockpit as admin but could not find the Maintain application tile. I'm using the SAP provided system for learning purpose.

Can you please help me out.

Thanks,

Praveen

entry.title: What is SAP plan to meet The Procurement act 2023 UK legislation requirements?
entry.content:
The new Procurement Act will be enforced in the UK from Oct 2024. According to the legislation, there will be a central digital platform for suppliers to register and store their details so that they can be used for multiple bids, and see all opportunities in one place. Simplified bidding processes will make it easier to bid, negotiate and work in partnership with the public sector.

In that case, bids will take place on the government digital platform rather than Ariba Sourcing. Is there a plan to connect to this digital platform?

entry.title: Goods Return without GRPO
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have a question and I would like your help .

For example, I want to return an item to the supplier, but this item contains several GRPO documents and I want to return it.

For example, I have 3 goods receipt documents (GRPO), and each one of them contains this item. Let us assume that the first receipt contains 3 pieces. The second receipt is 6 pieces, the third receipt is 8 pieces, and I want to return 9 pieces to the supplier. How do I do that?

In the normal situation I make a return document based on one of the receipt documents, but in this scenario I have several receipt documents... And if I make a return document without depended on a receipt document, then make Copy the receipt documents to AP in this case will require the total quantity of the receipt document without reducing the return.

How can I deal with this problem?

entry.title: Re: How to Reduce Database DTU on SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Here is how to identify the top wait categories from query store:

SELECT
    wait_category_desc,
    sum(total_query_wait_time_ms) as total_query_wait_time_ms
FROM
    sys.query_store_wait_stats qsws INNER JOIN
    sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval qsrsi ON qsws.runtime_stats_interval_id = qsrsi.runtime_stats_interval_id
WHERE
    qsrsi.start_time >= '2024-07-01 4:00:00' AND qsrsi.end_time <= '2024-07-01 5:00:00'
GROUP BY wait_category_desc
ORDER BY total_query_wait_time_ms DESC

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hi,

indeed, i have all i need out here, maybe it wouldn't be so if we're not all connected that well online.
As a maintenance user, i am mostly looking for answers, advices, geting a clear picture on how some of the processes work that haven't been explained better before. User experience mostly

entry.title: The Grand Prix of Developer Feedback
entry.content:
Dear fans of collaboration,

For over twenty-four months, the "SAP Help Portal" team, with their Open Documentation Initiative, has been leading the Formula 1 of developer documentation feedback. Bolstered by the robust support from the "Open Source" pit crew and a well-oiled GitHub collaboration process, it remains the Ferrari of feedback collection, driving continuous improvements on the documentation track. But does every spectator want to be in the Ferrari's driving seat? This season, we're putting that question to the test by introducing a rival racer: The comments feature on SAP Help Portal .

In other words... - results from a survey, but also the amount of feedback we received suggest that GitHub may not be the tool of choice to provide feedback for all roles of SAP BTP. For developers who use GitHub anyway it's a piece of cake to use it for feedback as well, sure, but administrators and especially business users might not be familiar with GitHub at all and hesitant to create an account just for that one purpose. 

For some guides you may have already noticed the following feedback options:

Menus for on SAP Help PortalUsagePrerequisite
Feedback > Create issue
Feedback > Edit page		Provide feedback through GitHubYou have a personal GitHub account
Feedback > CommentsProvide feedback on SAP Help PortalYou have a user on SAP Help Portal

 

This year, we'd like to compare the two approaches. And it's all about: measure, measure, measure. The guides below offer both options on SAP Help Portal. The best way to 'vote' for one option is to provide feedback using your most prefered feedback channel. 

RepositoryGuide
btp-best-practices-guideBest Practices for SAP BTP
btp-build-work-zone-advancedSAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
btp-business-application-studioSAP Business Application Studio
btp-cloud-loggingSAP Cloud Logging
btp-cloud-platformSAP Business Technology Platform
btp-credential-storeSAP Credential Store
btp-developer-guideSAP BTP Developer's Guide
btp-document-management-serviceSAP Document Management Service
btp-document-management-serviceUsing SAP Document Management Service Applications
btp-fiori-toolsSAP Fiori Tools User Guide
btp-identity-authenticationSAP Cloud Identity Services
btp-neo-environmentSAP BTP, Neo Environment
btp-private-link-serviceSAP Private Link Service
cloud-alm-setup-admin-guideSetup and Administration for SAP Cloud ALM
sap-artificial-intelligenceSAP AI Core
sap-artificial-intelligenceSAP AI Launchpad
sap-datasphereAcquiring, Preparing, and Modeling Data with SAP Datasphere
sap-datasphereAdministering SAP Datasphere
sap-datasphereIntegrating Data and Managing Spaces in SAP Datasphere
sap-hana-cloud-data-intelligenceABAP Integration Guide
sap-hana-cloud-data-intelligenceFunction Reference
sap-hana-cloud-data-intelligenceMachine Learning Guide
sap-hana-cloud-data-intelligenceModeling Guide
sap-hana-cloud-data-intelligenceRepository Objects Reference
sap-hana-cloud-data-lakeSAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake SQL Reference for Data Lake Relational Engine
sap-hana-cloud-data-lakeSAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake SQL Reference for Data Lake Relational Engine (SAP HANA DB-Managed)
sap-hana-cloud-hana-databaseSAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Deployment Infrastructure (HDI) Reference
sap-hana-cloud-hana-databaseSAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Developer Guide for Cloud Foundry Multitarget Applications (SAP Business App Studio)
sap-hana-cloud-hana-databaseSAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Developer Guide for Cloud Foundry Multitarget Applications (SAP Web IDE Full-Stack)
sap-hana-cloud-hana-databaseSAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL Reference Guide

We're always happy to receive your feedback, no matter through which channel. So it's really up to you if you prefer comments or GitHub. If you have a preference, I'd be interested to understand better why you prefer one or the other. Anyway, thank you for taking part in this race. 

Cheers,
Jens
